title: Bitfarms Reports Q2 2021 Financial Results and Establishes At-The-Market Equity Program
text: Bitfarms reported Q2 2021 revenues of $36.7 million, gross profit of $23.4 million (64% gross margin), operating loss of $2.1 million (-6% operating margin), and net loss of $3.7 million. The company mined 759 Bitcoin with average cost of approximately $9,000 per Bitcoin and held 1,293 Bitcoin valued at approximately $45.3 million as of June 30, 2021. Adjusted EBITDA of $23.8 million (65% adjusted EBITDA margin) was reported for the quarter. The company established an At-The-Market (ATM) equity program, enabling issuance of up to a specified amount of common shares for working capital and general corporate purposes without underwriting commissions. This capital-raising program supported expansion of mining operations and provided financial flexibility for growth initiatives. The ATM program represented management's confidence in Bitcoin price trajectory and demand for mining operations. Q2 2021 results demonstrated strong operating cash generation from mining operations despite net loss from non-cash charges. Management guidance indicated continued mining capacity expansion and potential for sustained profitability as Bitcoin prices remained elevated.
